choiceresponse.label:
Consider a patient who satisfies:
thal_2 ≤ 0.5
oldpeak = 0.21
cp_0 = 0.6Which statements are true? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: This patient follows the left branch at the root.
This patient reaches a leaf node predicting No Disease.
This patient’s prediction depends only on thal_2.
The prediction is determined by evaluating several conditions in sequence.
Features not encountered along this path (e.g., age) do not affect the prediction.

div.p: To determine the prediction of a decision tree for a specific patient, we start at the root node and evaluate each split condition using the patient’s feature values. Since this patient satisfies the root condition on thal_2, they follow the left branch. From there, additional conditions (such as on oldpeak and cp_0) are evaluated sequentially, guiding the patient down a single path until a leaf node is reached. That leaf node outputs a prediction of No Disease. Only the features that appear along this path influence the prediction; features that are never tested (such as age) have no effect on the outcome. This illustrates that decision tree explanations are local, path-dependent, and based on an ordered sequence of conditions rather than a single feature alone.
